channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats investigate DOJ for dropping charges on Mayor Adams
item.description: The lawmakers wrote a letter to Attorney General Pam Bondi on Sunday, accusing the DOJ of trying to cover up a quid pro quo.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: Canada may halt electricity exports to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Premier Doug Ford has threatened to cut off electricity exports to the US if President Trump's tariffs are enacted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans advised to avoid in-person town halls amid protests
item.description: House Republicans are being encouraged to avoid in-person town halls in favor of phone and livestreamed versions, advice that comes after constituents and activists rocked recent GOP events by expressing outrage about the Trump administration’s dramatic moves to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: BlackRock strikes deal to bring ports on both sides of Panama Canal under American control
item.description: A Hong Kong-based conglomerate has agreed to sell its controlling stake in a subsidiary that operates ports near the Panama Canal to a consortium including BlackRock Inc.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang And Alex Veiga, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In polarized times, these Minnesota legislators want to restore civility at the state Capitol
item.description: This year’s legislative session started with a lot of vitriol between DFLers and Republicans. But a bipartisan group of legislators is trying to get both sides to be more collaborative and civil.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says 25% tariffs on Mexican and Canadian imports will start Tuesday, with 'no room' for delay
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that 25% taxes on imports from Mexico and Canada would start Tuesday, sparking renewed fears of a North American trade war that already showed signs of pushing up inflation and hindering growth. “Tomorrow — tariffs 25% on Canada and 25% on Mexico.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ZEKE MILLER and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms McMahon to lead Education Department as Trump pushes to shut it down
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate voted Monday to confirm former wrestling executive Linda McMahon as the nation’s education chief, a role that places her atop a department that President Donald Trump has vilified and vowed to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's change in rhetoric signals push for peace: James Jeffrey
item.description: President Donald Trump's change in rhetoric signals an inevitable peace deal, according to former Ambassador James Jeffrey.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Today in history: 'Star-Spangled Banner' made official national anthem
item.description: It’s sung at athletic and political events alike — and Monday, "The Star-Spangled Banner” marks 94 years of being America’s national anthem.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants Palestinians out of Gaza. Here are Egypt’s plans to keep them there
item.description: Arab leaders will meet in Cairo to reject Palestinian displacement from Gaza. NPR looks at Egypt’s reconstruction plans for Gaza that counter President Trump’s ideas of expulsion.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy, MPR News

item.title: Trump again attacks Zelenskyy with potential peace deal in limbo
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Sanctuary city mayors to testify before House Oversight Committee
item.description: Democratic mayors from four sanctuary cities are preparing to testify before the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the U.S., British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as he prepared to host a summit of European leaders.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: ACLU and other advocates sue to block migrants from being sent to Guantánamo Bay
item.description: The lawsuit says there is no legitimate reason to send migrants to Guantánamo because the U.S. has ample detention facility.
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: A judge has continued to block President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth. U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King in Seattle issued the permanent injunction late Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto slams Trump administration cuts amid bird flu response
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto criticized the Trump administration Thursday, saying recent terminations are worsening the federal government’s response to the bird flu outbreak and the rise price of eggs.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
